generatedSentence: In a secluded neighborhood where felicity often felt like an occlusion, the peripatetic Reverend Elias maintained meticulous bookkeeping, his honesty a stark contrast to the waste he observed. He felt wearied by the town's slow decay, despite having been elected to a council tasked to kickstart change. Nightly, he watched the firework displays from his doorstep, an idealized vision of what the community could be, fearing a nutjob was trying to rearrange the very matrix of their lives, making any racing toward progress a gargantuan task. Suddenly, an offroad vehicle, probably one of the jeeps he often saw by the brooks, screeched to a halt, kicking up ground. This was the theatrical kickoff to a bizarre scenario. A shadowy figure emerged, a notorious hooligan, aptly described in police jargon, known for his formidable knife talent. The incoming threat caused a momentary blacken of Elias's vision, a bewilderment as profound as facing an occult ritual. He understood, with a painful jab, that his policy of gentle influence had failed, leaving him unblessed by luck. The figure was a kidnapper, not after riches, but after influence. Elias was forced into the vehicle's cockpit, feeling completely weightless, like a balloon unmoored. They navigated an underground passage, a trench leading to a coastal hideout, its landscape scarred by recent grading. The sheer impressiveness of the hideout's construction, fortified with wolfram, made his stomach scratch with anxiety. Watching the screen, he understood his captorsβÄô goal was to crucify his reputation, isolating him from his friends, making him feel like a mortal. He had to kindly navigate this ordeal, using his wit like finely honed blades, recalling his memoirs of past conflicts to find justice. He discussed his plight with his captor, generously sharing chocolate, a peace offering. He whiffed garlic from a nearby herbalistβÄôs preparation, a strange aroma mixing with the metallic scent of old lumber. He knew he had to unpack the truth, faster than they could rearrange the narrative, to justify his actions and avoid being jailed. It was a race against time to upgrade his situation, lest he become another grim entry in the undertakersβÄô ledger, leaving his microbiome as his only legacy.

He felt wearied by the town's slow decay, despite having been elected to a council tasked to kickstart change. Nightly, he watched the firework displays from his doorstep, an idealized vision of what the community could be, fearing a nutjob was trying to rearrange the very matrix of their lives, making any racing toward progress a gargantuan task.

Suddenly, an offroad vehicle, probably one of the jeeps he often saw by the brooks, screeched to a halt, kicking up ground. This was the theatrical kickoff to a bizarre scenario. A shadowy figure emerged, a notorious hooligan, aptly described in police jargon, known for his formidable knife talent. The incoming threat caused a momentary blacken of Elias's vision, a bewilderment as profound as facing an occult ritual. He understood, with a painful jab, that his policy of gentle influence had failed, leaving him unblessed by luck.

The figure was a kidnapper, not after riches, but after influence. Elias was forced into the vehicle's cockpit, feeling completely weightless, like a balloon unmoored. They navigated an underground passage, a trench leading to a coastal hideout, its landscape scarred by recent grading. The sheer impressiveness of the hideout's construction, fortified with wolfram, made his stomach scratch with anxiety. Watching the screen, he understood his captorsβÄô goal was to crucify his reputation, isolating him from his friends, making him feel like a mortal.

He had to kindly navigate this ordeal, using his wit like finely honed blades, recalling his memoirs of past conflicts to find justice. He discussed his plight with his captor, generously sharing chocolate, a peace offering. He whiffed garlic from a nearby herbalistβÄôs preparation, a strange aroma mixing with the metallic scent of old lumber. He knew he had to unpack the truth, faster than they could rearrange the narrative, to justify his actions and avoid being jailed. It was a race against time to upgrade his situation, lest he become another grim entry in the undertakersβÄô ledger, leaving his microbiome as his only legacy.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. PROTAGONIST: Reverend Elias, a weathered man with a distinctive silver pompadour and a tweed suit, carrying an old leather-bound ledger. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Reverend Elias stands on his doorstep, silhouetted by explosive fireworks in the night sky, looking out at a distorted, shimmering city matrix. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A bulky offroad jeep with glowing headlights screeches to a halt, kicking up dust, a menacing figure with a large, glinting knife stepping out into a spotlight. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias, now in the jeep's cockpit, looking bewildered and weightless, as the vehicle plunges into a dark, futuristic underground passage. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, calm but determined, offers a piece of chocolate to a shadowy captor across a table, a faint garlic aroma swirling in the air, a glowing data screen in the background.

definitions.definition: To change the order or arrangement of (one or more items).
Happiness.
An apt and pleasing style in speech, writing, etc.
(semiology) Reproduction of a sign with fidelity.
definitions.example: The quotation was rendered with felicity.

definitions.definition: Something that is either a source of happiness or particularly apt.
The quality of being a neighbor, of living nearby, next to each-other; proximity.
definitions.example: Our neighborhood was our only reason to exchange hollow greetings.

definitions.definition: Close proximity, nearby area; particularly, close proximity to one's home.
definitions.example: He lives in my neighborhood.

definitions.definition: The inhabitants of a residential area.
definitions.example: The fire alarmed the neighborhood.

definitions.definition: A formal or informal division of a municipality or region.
definitions.example: We have just moved to a pleasant neighborhood.

definitions.definition: An approximate amount.
definitions.example: He must be making in the neighborhood of $200,000 per year.

definitions.definition: The quality of physical proximity.
definitions.example: The slums and the palace were in awful neighborhood.

definitions.definition: The disposition becoming a neighbor; neighborly kindness or good will.
Within a topological space:
Within a metric space:
The infinitesimal open set of all points that may be reached directly from a given point.
The set of all the vertices adjacent to a given vertex.
The process of occluding, or something that occludes.
Anything that obstructs or closes a vessel or canal.
The alignment of the teeth when upper and lower jaws are brought together.
An occluded front.
A closure within the vocal tract that produces an oral stop or nasal stop.
The absorption of a gas or liquid by a substance such as a metal.
The blocking of the view of part of an image by another.
Designed for, used for, or taking place on a driving surface that is not traditionally paved, such as a beach or rugged terrain
Not situated on a street; e.g. off-street parking, in contrast to parking on a street.
To use; enjoy; have the full employment of.
To earn; deserve.
To bear; endure; support; put up with; tolerate (usually used in the negative, with an abstract noun as object).
definitions.example: I will not brook any disobedience. βÄÉ I will brook no refusal. βÄÉ I will brook no impertinence.

definitions.definition: A body of running water smaller than a river; a small stream.
A water meadow.
(in the plural) Low, marshy ground.
To do bookkeeping.
The skill or practice of keeping books or systematic records of financial transactions, e.g. income and expenses.
General tasks for maintaining a system.
The act, quality, or condition of being honest.
definitions.example: academic / artistic / emotional / intellectual honesty

definitions.definition: Honor; decency, propriety.
Chastity.
Any of various crucifers in the genus Lunaria, several of which are grown as ornamentals, particularly Lunaria annua.
One who walks about; a pedestrian; an itinerant.
(usually capitalized) One who accepts the philosophy of Aristotle or his school; an Aristotelian.
Tending to walk about.
Constantly travelling; itinerant; nomadic.
(usually capitalized) Having to do with Aristotle, his philosophy, or the school of thought which he founded -- from the practice of conducting philosophical conversations while taking a walk.
To make or to become weary.
Excess of material, useless by-products or damaged, unsaleable products; garbage; rubbish.
Excrement or urine.
definitions.example: The cage was littered with animal waste

definitions.definition: A waste land; an uninhabited desolate region; a wilderness or desert.
A place that has been laid waste or destroyed.
A large tract of uncultivated land.
The part of the land of a manor (of whatever size) not used for cultivation or grazing, nowadays treated as common land.
A vast expanse of water.
A disused mine or part of one.
The action or progress of wasting; extravagant consumption or ineffectual use.
definitions.example: Her life seemed a waste

definitions.definition: Large abundance of something, specifically without it being used.
Gradual loss or decay.
A decaying of the body by disease; wasting away.
Destruction or devastation caused by war or natural disasters; See "to lay waste"

A cause of action which may be brought by the owner of a future interest in property against the current owner of that property to prevent the current owner from degrading the value or character of the property, either intentionally or through neglect.
Material derived by mechanical and chemical erosion from the land, carried by streams to the sea.
A small, blocky, military-style vehicle with four-wheel drive, suited to rough terrain.
A light utility truck from WWII used by the U.S. Army, and subsequently turned into the trademark Jeep when civilianized by originating manufacturer Willys-Overland.
(genericized trademark) A sport utility vehicle or similar four-wheel-drive vehicle.
(corrosion) A device for detecting discontinuity in a protective coating, also called a "holiday detector"
A covered shopping cart, also called a "shopping stroller".
To choose or make a decision (to do something)
To choose (a candidate) in an election
One who is elected.
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
A stage performance, especially one by amateurs.
A commercially produced film to be shown in movie theaters.
Of or relating to the theatre.
Fake and exaggerated.
The womb.
The material or tissue in which more specialized structures are embedded.
An extracellular matrix, the material or tissue between the cells of animals or plants.
Part of the mitochondrion.
The medium in which bacteria are cultured.

A rectangular arrangement of numbers or terms having various uses such as transforming coordinates in geometry, solving systems of linear equations in linear algebra and representing graphs in graph theory.
A two-dimensional array.
A grid-like arrangement of electronic components, especially one intended for information coding, decoding or storage.
A table of data.
A geological matrix.
(archaeology and paleontology) The sediment surrounding and including the artifacts, features, and other materials at a site.
The environment from which a given sample is taken.
In hot metal typesetting, a mold for casting a letter.
In printmaking, the plate or block used, with ink, to hold the image that makes up the print.
The cavity or mold in which anything is formed.
(dyeing) The five simple colours (black, white, blue, red, and yellow) from which all the others are formed.
A binding agent of composite materials, e.g. resin in fibreglass.
To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).
definitions.example: The drivers were racing around the track.

definitions.definition: To compete against in such a race.
definitions.example: I raced him to the car, but he was there first, so he got to ride shotgun.

definitions.definition: To move or drive at high speed; to hurry or speed.
definitions.example: As soon as it was time to go home, he raced for the door.

definitions.definition: Of a motor, to run rapidly when not engaged to a transmission.
To assign a race to; to perceive as having a (usually specified) race.
The sport of competing in races.
To demolish; to level to the ground.
To scrape as if with a razor.
A member of the Christian clergy; a minister.
Worthy of reverence or respect; reverent.

A device using gunpowder and other chemicals which, when lit, emits a combination of coloured flames, sparks, whistles or bangs, and sometimes made to rocket high into the sky before exploding, used for entertainment or celebration.
definitions.example: Commercial firework displays are commonly launched from boats or other vessels

definitions.definition: Someone who is crazy (insane).

definitions.definition: A big slice, especially of bread.

A site of conflict; a battlefield.
The vagina.

A well, usually near the stern, where the helm is located.
An area from where something is controlled or managed; a centre of control.
A food made from ground roasted cocoa beans.

A single, small piece of confectionery made from chocolate.

definitions.definition: A dark, reddish-brown colour/color, like that of chocolate (also called chocolate brown).

definitions.definition: A black person; blackness.

To treat blood agar by heating in order to lyse the red blood cells in the medium.
Made of or containing chocolate.
Having a dark reddish-brown colour/color.
Black (relating to any of various ethnic groups having dark pigmentation of the skin).
(causative) To cause to be or become black.
To become black.

definitions.definition: (causative) To make dirty.
To defame or sully.
To cook (meat or fish) by coating with pepper, etc., and quickly searing in a hot pan.

definitions.definition: Part of a path or journey.

definitions.definition: An incident or episode.

definitions.definition: The advance of time.

A passageway or corridor.
An underground cavity, formed by water or falling rocks, which is much longer than it is wide.
The vagina.
The act of passing; movement across or through.
The right to pass from one place to another.

Serial passage, a technique used in bacteriology and virology
A gambling game for two players using three dice, in which the object is to throw a double over ten.
To pass something, such as a pathogen or stem cell, through a host or medium

definitions.definition: To make a passage, especially by sea; to cross

definitions.definition: Describing a bird that has left the nest, is living on its own, but is less than a year old. (commonly used in falconry)

To organize in grades.

Grammar school, primary school, or the years of school prior to high school.
A professional person qualified (as by a law degree or bar exam) and authorized to practice law, i.e. represent parties in lawsuits or trials and give legal advice.

The burbot.
The stem of a bramble.
To practice law.

To make legalistic arguments.
To barrage (a person) with questions in order to get them to admit something.

An act of starting a motorcycle by quickly depressing the kick start with one's foot.

To start a motorcycle.

definitions.definition: To start or begin strongly or quickly.

definitions.definition: To make (something) active, functional, or productive again; to reinvigorate (an activity, system, or process).
definitions.example: To kick start the economy with a large construction project.

definitions.definition: A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A liquid content (e.g. oil) which acts as a binding and drying agent in paint. (FM 55-501).

An entity to achieve an end.

An animal or (rarely) a plant on which a Hindu deity rides or sits
In all likelihood.
Of the giant Gargantua or his appetite.
Huge; immense; tremendous.

A rapid transit system, regardless of the elevation of its right of way.
An underground walkway, tunnel for pedestrians (called pedestrian underpass in US).
An underground route for pipes, sewers, etc.
Regions beneath the surface of the earth, both natural (eg. caves) and man-made (eg. mines).
(with definite article) A movement or organisation of people who resist political convention.

Below the ground; below the surface of the Earth.

definitions.definition: Below the ground.

definitions.definition: Secretly.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.

definitions.example: He enjoyed the simple pleasure of watching the kickline kick.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.
definitions.example: He was kicked by ChanServ for flooding.

definitions.definition: To overcome (a bothersome or difficult issue or obstacle); to free oneself of (a problem).
definitions.example: I still smoke, but they keep telling me to kick the habit.

definitions.definition: To move or push suddenly and violently.

definitions.definition: (of a firearm) To recoil; to push by recoiling.
To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.
To accelerate quickly with a few pedal strokes in an effort to break away from other riders.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
The action of the verb to kick.
definitions.example: In boxing, kicking one's opponent is not allowed.

definitions.definition: A violent assault involving repeated kicks.

definitions.definition: A great loss or defeat; licking.
Terrific, great (of clothes) smart, fashionable.

definitions.definition: Alive, active (especially in the phrase alive and kicking).

definitions.definition: (of a party or event) Actively ongoing and enjoyable.

A utensil or a tool designed for cutting, consisting of a flat piece of hard material, usually steel or other metal (the blade), usually sharpened on one edge, attached to a handle. The blade may be pointed for piercing.
A weapon designed with the aforementioned specifications intended for slashing and/or stabbing and too short to be called a sword. A dagger.
Any blade-like part in a tool or a machine designed for cutting, such as that of a chipper.
To cut with a knife.
To use a knife to injure or kill by stabbing, slashing, or otherwise using the sharp edge of the knife as a weapon.
To cut through as if with a knife.
To betray, especially in the context of a political slate.
To positively ignore, especially in order to denigrate. compare cut
A marked natural ability or skill.
definitions.example: He has a real talent for drawing.

definitions.definition: A unit of weight and money used in ancient times in Greece, the Roman Empire, and the Middle East.
A desire or inclination for something.
People of talent, viewed collectively; a talented person.

definitions.definition: The men or (especially) women of a place or area, judged by their attractiveness.
definitions.example: Not much talent in this bar tonightβÄîlet's hit the clubs.

definitions.definition: Any plant.
A plant raised for some edible part of it, such as the leaves, roots, fruit or flowers, but excluding any plant considered to be a fruit, grain, herb, or spice in the culinary sense.
The edible part of such a plant.
A person whose brain (or, infrequently, body) has been damaged so that they cannot interact with the surrounding environment; a person in a persistent vegetative state.
A reduction in consumer cost (usually for a limited time) for items or services rendered.
definitions.example: We're running a special on turkey for Thanksgiving.

definitions.definition: One of a rotation of meals systematically offered for a lower price at a restaurant.

definitions.definition: Unusual or exceptional episode of a series.

definitions.definition: A special constable.
Anything that is not according to normal practice, plan, or schedule, as an unscheduled run of transportation that is normally scheduled.
definitions.example: Thousands came to see the special that carried the President's coffin.

definitions.definition: Any unlicensed medicine produced or obtained for a specific individual patient.
A correspondent; a journalist sent to the scene of an event to report back.
A dispatch sent back by a special correspondent.
A light that illuminates a specific person or thing on the stage.
(nursing) To supervise a patient one-on-one.
Distinguished by a unique or unusual quality.
definitions.example: a special episode of a television series

definitions.definition: Of particular personal interest or value; dear; beloved.
definitions.example: Everyone is special to someone.

definitions.definition: Of or related to learning or intellectual disabilities.
definitions.example: He goes to a special school.

definitions.definition: Constituting or relating to a species.
definitions.example: The seven dark spots is a special property unique to Coccinella septempunctata.

definitions.definition: Chief in excellence.
Of or related to unconventional warfare, as in "special forces" and "special operations".
To confuse, disorientate, or puzzle someone, especially with many different choices.
definitions.example: All the different possible options may bewilder us.

definitions.definition: A violent and noisy football (soccer) fan who routinely fights with supporters of opposing teams, often the member of a firm.
A person that causes trouble or violence.
(Navy slang) A member of the coast guard.
A type of fish, an anadromous smelt, Thaleichthys pacificus, found in the North Pacific.
Wolframite
Tungsten
To waft.
To sniff.
To strike out.
To miss the ball completely.
To attempt to strike and miss, especially being off-balance/vulnerable after missing.
To throw out in whiffs; to consume in whiffs; to puff.
To carry or convey by a whiff, or as by a whiff; to puff or blow away.
To have or give off a strong, unpleasant smell.
To fail spectacularly at a task.
To fish with a handline.
The terminal point of something in space or time.
definitions.example: At the end of the road, turn left.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The cessation of an effort, activity, state, or motion.
definitions.example: Is there no end to this madness?

definitions.definition: (by extension) Death.
definitions.example: He met a terrible end in the jungle.

definitions.definition: The most extreme point of an object, especially one that is longer than it is wide.
definitions.example: Hold the string at both ends.

definitions.definition: Result.
A purpose, goal, or aim.

Definitions related to standing in the path of an opposing player
An erection of white canvas or wood placed on the boundary opposite a batsman to make the ball more easily visible.
A collection of less-valuable vessels that travel with a more valuable one for the latter's protection.
A dwarf wall or partition carried up to a certain height for separation and protection, as in a church, to separate the aisle from the choir, etc.
A large scarf.
To filter by passing through a screen.
definitions.example: Mary screened the beans to remove the clumps of gravel.

definitions.definition: To shelter or conceal.
To remove information, or censor intellectual material from viewing.
definitions.example: The news report was screened because it accused the politician of wrongdoing.

definitions.definition: To present publicly (on the screen).
definitions.example: The news report will be screened at 11:00 tonight.

definitions.definition: To fit with a screen.
definitions.example: We need to screen this porch. These bugs are driving me crazy.

definitions.definition: To examine patients or treat a sample in order to detect a chemical or a disease, or to assess susceptibility to a disease.
To search chemical libraries by means of a computational technique in order to identify chemical compounds which would potentially bind to a given biological target such as a protein.
To stand so as to block a defender from reaching a teammate.
To determine the source or subject matter of a call before deciding whether to answer the phone.
Any American marsupial of the family Didelphidae. The common species of the United States is Didelphis virginiana.
To cast a spell upon.
To fascinate or charm.
definitions.example: I was bewitched by the sight of the girl dancing in the forest.

definitions.definition: To astonish, amaze.
Under a spell; entranced.
A principle of behaviour, conduct etc. thought to be desirable or necessary, especially as formally expressed by a government or other authoritative body.
definitions.example: The Communist Party has a policy of returning power to the workers.

definitions.definition: Wise or advantageous conduct; prudence, formerly also with connotations of craftiness.
Specifically, political shrewdness or (formerly) cunning; statecraft.
(now chiefly in the plural) The grounds of a large country house.
The art of governance; political science.
A state; a polity.
A set political system; civil administration.
A trick; a stratagem.
Motive; object; inducement.
To regulate by laws; to reduce to order.
(of she-dog, she-wolf, vixen, etc.) To give birth.
definitions.example: The bitch whelped.

definitions.definition: A quick stab or blow; a poking or thrusting motion.
A short straight punch.
A medical injection.
definitions.example: Our dog was exposed to rabies, so the whole family went to a clinic to get our jabs.

definitions.definition: A vaccination, whether or not delivered via conventional injection.
A mild verbal insult.
To poke or thrust abruptly, or to make such a motion.
To deliver a quick punch.
To give someone an injection
Having a justification.
definitions.example: The act was fully justified.

definitions.definition: Of text, arranged on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for oneβÄôs actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.
definitions.example: She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
To imprison.
The state or characteristic of being just or fair.
definitions.example: the justice of a description

definitions.definition: The ideal of fairness, impartiality, etc., especially with regard to the punishment of wrongdoing.
definitions.example: Justice was served.

definitions.definition: Judgment and punishment of a party who has allegedly wronged another.
definitions.example: to demand justice

definitions.definition: The civil power dealing with law.
definitions.example: Ministry of Justice

definitions.definition: A title given to judges of certain courts; capitalized as a title.
definitions.example: Mr. Justice Krever presides over the appellate court

definitions.definition: Correctness, conforming to reality or rules.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
definitions.example: Look, I found a ten dollar bill on the ground!

definitions.definition: Terrain.
Soil, earth.
definitions.example: The fox escaped from the hounds by going to ground.

definitions.definition: The bottom of a body of water.
Basis, foundation, groundwork, legwork.
(chiefly in the plural) Reason, (epistemic) justification, cause.
definitions.example: He could not come on grounds of health, or on health grounds.

definitions.definition: Background, context, framework, surroundings.
The area on which a battle is fought, particularly as referring to the area occupied by one side or the other. Often, according to the eventualities, "to give ground" or "to gain ground".
(by extension) Advantage given or gained in any contest; e.g. in football, chess, debate or academic discourse.
The plain surface upon which the figures of an artistic composition are set.
definitions.example: crimson flowers on a white ground

definitions.definition: In sculpture, a flat surface upon which figures are raised in relief.
In point lace, the net of small meshes upon which the embroidered pattern is applied.
definitions.example: Brussels ground

definitions.definition: In etching, a gummy substance spread over the surface of a metal to be etched, to prevent the acid from eating except where an opening is made by the needle.
(chiefly in the plural) One of the pieces of wood, flush with the plastering, to which mouldings etc. are attached.
definitions.example: Grounds are usually put up first and the plastering floated flush with them.

definitions.definition: A soccer stadium.
definitions.example: Manchester United's ground is known as Old Trafford.

definitions.definition: An electrical conductor connected to the earth, or a large conductor whose electrical potential is taken as zero (such as a steel chassis).

The area of grass on which a match is played (a cricket field); the entire arena in which it is played; the part of the field behind a batsman's popping crease where he can not be run out (hence to make one's ground).
A composition in which the bass, consisting of a few bars of independent notes, is continually repeated to a varying melody.
The tune on which descants are raised; the plain song.
The pit of a theatre.
To connect (an electrical conductor or device) to a ground.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by forcing him/her to stay at home and/or give up certain privileges.
definitions.example: Eric, you are grounded until further notice for lying to us about where you were last night!

definitions.definition: To forbid (an aircraft or pilot) to fly.
definitions.example: Because of the bad weather, all flights were grounded.

definitions.definition: To give a basic education in a particular subject; to instruct in elements or first principles.
definitions.example: Jim was grounded in maths.

definitions.definition: To hit a ground ball. Compare fly (verb(regular)) and line (verb).
To place something on the ground.
To run aground; to strike the bottom and remain fixed.
definitions.example: The ship grounded on the bar.

definitions.definition: To found; to fix or set, as on a foundation, reason, or principle; to furnish a ground for; to fix firmly.
To cover with a ground, as a copper plate for etching, or as paper or other materials with a uniform tint as a preparation for ornament.
To improve or focus the mental or emotional state of.
definitions.example: I ground myself with meditation.
